item.title: 🇺🇦✈️ Ukrainian Mirage 2000s spotted flying air-to-ground strike missions
item.description: A low-level flight profile followed by a steep climb suggests French-supplied Mirage 2000s are now conducting ground-attack operations, expanding their combat role beyond air defense.

item.title: 🇷🇺🤖 Russian trucks receive dazzle paint to confuse AI-powered drones
item.description: Russian forces are applying disruptive paint schemes to military vehicles designed to confuse machine-vision targeting systems as autonomous drones become an increasing threat on the battlefield.

item.title: 🇹🇷📡 Turkey's secretive HAVA SOJ electronic warfare jet appears in new imagery
item.description: The long-delayed Turkish Air Force electronic warfare aircraft, designed to blind enemy air defenses from outside contested airspace, has been spotted in new imagery, signaling progress on the indigenous program.

item.title: 🔫🇺🇸 SOCOM seeks new flyweight machine guns and rifles for special operations
item.description: U.S. Special Operations Command is searching for a new 5.56mm flyweight assault machine gun to replace lightweight M249 variants, as part of a broader push to modernize small arms for special forces.

item.title: 🇺🇸🤖 MQ-28 Ghost Bat drone now flying over Pacific from U.S. Navy base
item.description: Boeing's Australian-born MQ-28 Ghost Bat autonomous combat drone is conducting test flights from a U.S. Navy base in California, demonstrating maturity and promoting potential Pentagon sales.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Fire and smoke greet Putin's Economic Forum as Ukraine strikes St. Petersburg oil terminal
item.description: The attack coincided with the beginning of the 2026 St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, an annual conference of business leaders and government officials hosted by Russian President Vladimir Putin.

item.title: 🇺🇦🤝 NATO chief arrives in Kyiv day after mass Russian strike
item.description: The visit comes only a day after one of Russia's heaviest attacks killed over 20 people and injured over 100 across Ukraine.

item.title: 🇷🇺⚔️ General Staff: Russia has lost 1,368,040 troops in Ukraine since Feb. 24, 2022
item.description: The number includes 1,130 casualties that Russian forces suffered over the past day.

item.title: 🇷🇺🔥 Russian weapons plant in flames after Ukrainian attack in Tambov Oblast
item.description: The JSC Progress Plant in Michurinsk, Tambov Oblast, produces high-tech aviation and missile control systems for Russia's military, along with equipment for gas and oil pipelines.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇦 Zelensky plans to attend G7 summit in France
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to join leaders of the Group of Seven nations at the June 15-17 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France.

item.title: 🇱🇹🇺🇸 Next US troop deployment to Lithuania remains 'under review'
item.description: "Because the number of (U.S. troops) in Europe is changing, this naturally leads to a review of regional stance," Lithuanian Defense Minister Robertas Kaunas said.

item.title: 🇺🇦🚢 World's largest shipping firm invests in Ukrainian port near Odesa despite war risks
item.description: The Swiss-headquartered Mediterranean Shipping Company acquired a stake in the TIS container terminal in Ukraine's Pivdennyi Port, one person with close knowledge of the deal confirmed to the Kyiv Independent.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU weighs future of Ukrainian refugees as some member states harden their stance
item.description: The EU is set to discuss extending Ukrainian refugee protections for a sixth time, amid thorny questions over whether and when more than 4 million refugees in Europe will return home.

item.title: 🇺🇦🚁 Neva radar, Pantsir air defense, and a tugboat — Ukraine releases latest drone strike hit list
item.description: Ukraine struck Russian military targets across occupied Crimea and Donetsk Oblast overnight, including two Pantsir air defense systems and a command post.

item.title: 🇱🇹🇺🇸 Future presence of US troops in Lithuania is 'under review'
item.description: U.S. troops presently stationed in the country are now leaving as expected, but the next scheduled group is not arriving, the defense minister said.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇮🇱 France restricts Israeli presence at Europe's biggest defense show
item.description: The restrictions come amid strained relations between Paris and Jerusalem over Israel's military operations in Lebanon.

item.title: 🇪🇺⚓ US, NATO allies to launch scaled-back Baltic Sea drills
item.description: Officials say the smaller footprint reflects operational realities rather than waning commitment.

item.title: 🇵🇱☢️ US may consider placing nukes in Poland, Baltic States
item.description: While the discussions were ongoing, an agreement to expand U.S. nuclear hosting was not imminent, an unnamed source told the FT.

item.title: 🇳🇴☢️ Norway becomes ninth country to sign up for French nuclear deterrence as trust in US falters
item.description: Norway joins European allies seeking alternative nuclear deterrence arrangements amid concerns about U.S. reliability.

item.title: 🇰🇷⚓ South Korea goes full steam ahead on nuclear-powered submarines
item.description: Seoul plans to launch its first boat in the mid-2030s and commission it in "the late 2030s," the government proclaimed.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Gulf hostilities flare as U.S. intercepts Iranian missiles, strikes Qeshm Island
item.description: Live updates: Kuwait suspends flights after Iranian drone strike on airport; U.S. Central Command says it conducted self-defense strikes on Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel's capitulation to Trump in Lebanon exposes its reliance on the U.S.
item.description: Analysis: Israel's global standing slips as it agrees to Trump's terms on Lebanon, revealing its dependence on U.S. support.

item.title: 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
item.description: Ethiopia has banned imports of gasoline-powered vehicles, pushing a rapid transition to electric vehicles in Africa.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Cockroach movement gives voice to India's angry youth
item.description: A grassroots protest movement in India is mobilizing young people frustrated with economic inequality and political corruption.
